name: Big-Batch Lentil Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Red or brown lentils, rinsed
2 cans Chicken broth, reduced sodium (46 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm the olive oil in a 10-quart pot over medium heat. Add the onions and cook for 3 minutes. Stir frequently.
2. Add the red bell peppers, garlic, jalapeno peppers, oregano, cumin, and coriander to the pot. Cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the vegetables are soft.
3. Stir in the lentils, chicken broth (broken up with a spoon), diced tomatoes, and 2 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 20-30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender. Stir in the salt.
4. Remove about one-third of the soup and puree it in a food processor fitted with a metal blade. Pulse on and off to maintain some texture. Stir the purée back into the soup pot along with the cilantro.
5. For the lime cream: In a small bowl, mix together the sour cream and lime juice.
Dinner Plan:
- Remove two-thirds of the soup (approximately 14 cups) and store in two separate microwave-safe containers for later use. Allow to cool before refrigerating or freezing.
- Rewarm the remaining soup before serving, if needed. Top with the lime cream just before serving. Approximate microwave times: Defrost for 45 minutes; Reheat for 9 minutes, stirring once or twice. Make fresh lime cream as desired.
